title: Peninsular Malaysia

infobox.value: or simply **Malaya** prior to [1963](/wiki/Malaysia_Agreement "Malaysia Agreement"),[[b]](#cite_note-2) and also known as **West Malaysia** and the **Malaysian Peninsula**,[[c]](#cite_note-3) is the southern part of the [Malay Peninsula](/wiki/Malay_Peninsula "Malay Peninsula") in [Mainland Southeast Asia](/wiki/Mainland_Southeast_Asia "Mainland Southeast Asia") along with the [nearby islands](/wiki/List_of_islands_of_Malaysia "List of islands of Malaysia") that are part of [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia").[[1]](#cite_note-4) Its area totals approximately 131,956 km2 (50,948 sq mi). It makes up nearly 40% of the total area of Malaysia; the other 60% is on the island of [Borneo](/wiki/Borneo "Borneo"), also known as [East Malaysia](/wiki/East_Malaysia "East Malaysia").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Western, mainland part of Malaysia

Not to be confused with [Malay Peninsula](/wiki/Malay_Peninsula "Malay Peninsula").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a1/LANDSAT_semenanjung_malaysia_-_2.jpg/250px-LANDSAT_semenanjung_malaysia_-_2.jpg)](/wiki/File:LANDSAT_semenanjung_malaysia_-_2.jpg)

Landsat false-colour mosaic of Peninsular Malaysia

**Peninsular Malaysia**,[[a]](#cite_note-1) historically known as **Peninsular Malaya** or simply **Malaya** prior to [1963](/wiki/Malaysia_Agreement "Malaysia Agreement"),[[b]](#cite_note-2) and also known as **West Malaysia** and the **Malaysian Peninsula**,[[c]](#cite_note-3) is the southern part of the [Malay Peninsula](/wiki/Malay_Peninsula "Malay Peninsula") in [Mainland Southeast Asia](/wiki/Mainland_Southeast_Asia "Mainland Southeast Asia") along with the [nearby islands](/wiki/List_of_islands_of_Malaysia "List of islands of Malaysia") that are part of [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia").[[1]](#cite_note-4) Its area totals approximately 131,956 km2 (50,948 sq mi). It makes up nearly 40% of the total area of Malaysia; the other 60% is on the island of [Borneo](/wiki/Borneo "Borneo"), also known as [East Malaysia](/wiki/East_Malaysia "East Malaysia").

The present-day land [border](/wiki/Border "Border") with [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand") to the north originates from the [Anglo–Siamese Treaty of 1909](/wiki/Anglo%E2%80%93Siamese_Treaty_of_1909 "Anglo–Siamese Treaty of 1909"), while a maritime border with [Singapore](/wiki/Singapore "Singapore") to the south was established following the [independence of Singapore](/wiki/Independence_of_Singapore_Agreement_1965 "Independence of Singapore Agreement 1965") in 1965.[[2]](#cite_note-5) To the west, across the [Strait of Malacca](/wiki/Strait_of_Malacca "Strait of Malacca"), lies the island of [Sumatra](/wiki/Sumatra "Sumatra"), and to the east, across the [South China Sea](/wiki/South_China_Sea "South China Sea"), lie the [Natuna Islands](/wiki/Natuna_Islands "Natuna Islands"), both part of [Indonesia](/wiki/Indonesia "Indonesia"). At its southern tip, across the [Strait of Johor](/wiki/Strait_of_Johor "Strait of Johor"), lies the [island country](/wiki/Island_country "Island country") of [Singapore](/wiki/Singapore "Singapore"). Most of Peninsular Malaysia's interior is forested, mountainous and rural, while the majority of the population and economic activity are concentrated along the coastal western half, where the country's major urban centres are located.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ef/Map_PeninsularMalaysia.png/250px-Map_PeninsularMalaysia.png)](/wiki/File:Map_PeninsularMalaysia.png)

Map of Peninsular Malaysia

## States and federal territories

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Peninsular_Malaysia&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: States and federal territories")]

Peninsular Malaysia consists of 11 out of the 13 [states](/wiki/States_and_federal_territories_of_Malaysia "States and federal territories of Malaysia"), and two out of the three [federal territories](/wiki/Federal_Territories_of_Malaysia "Federal Territories of Malaysia") of Malaysia, which includes the national capital of [Kuala Lumpur](/wiki/Kuala_Lumpur "Kuala Lumpur"). The states are listed as the following:

    | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
    | [Flag of Johor](/wiki/Flag_of_Johor "Flag of Johor") | [Coat of arms of Johor](/wiki/Coat_of_arms_of_Johor "Coat of arms of Johor") | [Johor](/wiki/Johor "Johor") | [Johor Bahru](/wiki/Johor_Bahru "Johor Bahru") | [Muar](/wiki/Muar_(town) "Muar (town)") | 19,166 | [Sultan](/wiki/Sultan_of_Johor "Sultan of Johor") | [Menteri Besar](/wiki/List_of_Menteris_Besar_of_Johor "List of Menteris Besar of Johor") |
    | [Flag of Kedah](/wiki/Flag_of_Kedah "Flag of Kedah") | [Coat of arms of Kedah](/wiki/Coat_of_arms_of_Kedah "Coat of arms of Kedah") | [Kedah](/wiki/Kedah "Kedah") | [Alor Setar](/wiki/Alor_Setar "Alor Setar") | [Anak Bukit](/wiki/Anak_Bukit "Anak Bukit") | 9,492 | [Sultan](/wiki/Kedah_Sultanate "Kedah Sultanate") | [Menteri Besar](/wiki/List_of_Menteris_Besar_of_Kedah "List of Menteris Besar of Kedah") |
    | [Flag of Kelantan](/wiki/Flag_of_Kelantan "Flag of Kelantan") | [Coat of arms of Kelantan](/wiki/Coat_of_arms_of_Kelantan "Coat of arms of Kelantan") | [Kelantan](/wiki/Kelantan "Kelantan") | [Kota Bharu](/wiki/Kota_Bharu "Kota Bharu") | [Kubang Kerian](/wiki/Kubang_Kerian "Kubang Kerian") | 15,040 | [Sultan](/wiki/Sultan_of_Kelantan "Sultan of Kelantan") | [Menteri Besar](/wiki/List_of_Menteris_Besar_of_Kelantan "List of Menteris Besar of Kelantan") |

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Peninsular_Malaysia&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Etymology")]

See also: [Malaysia § Etymology](/wiki/Malaysia#Etymology "Malaysia")

Originally comprising the states and territories of the [Federation of Malaya](/wiki/Federation_of_Malaya "Federation of Malaya"), the then Federation under the [Malaysia Agreement](/wiki/Malaysia_Agreement "Malaysia Agreement") merged with the [Crown Colony of North Borneo](/wiki/Crown_Colony_of_North_Borneo "Crown Colony of North Borneo"), the [Crown Colony of Sarawak](/wiki/Crown_Colony_of_Sarawak "Crown Colony of Sarawak") and the self-governing [State of Singapore](/wiki/Colony_of_Singapore "Colony of Singapore") to form the new Federation called [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia"). The merger was initially proposed in order to reunify Singapore with its [hinterland](/wiki/Hinterland "Hinterland") in the Federation as they were originally associated under [British Malaya](/wiki/British_Malaya "British Malaya") but later separated and were governed separately after the formation of the [Malayan Union](/wiki/Malayan_Union "Malayan Union"). Even when the Malayan Union transformed into the Federation of Malaya, Singapore was not a part of it. Although politically distinct, Malaya was then seen geographically as comprising the States of the Federation of Malaya in the Peninsula and Singapore. In order to facilitate the merger, the Borneo States (which initially also included [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei")) were brought in as well as it was believed that with the inclusion of the various ethnic groups in Borneo, the racial arithmetic would be offset such that the influx of ethnic Chinese from Singapore would not politically overwhelm Malaya, satisfying the [Malay ultras](/wiki/Ultra_(Malaysia) "Ultra (Malaysia)").

Ultimately, both Malaysia and Singapore agreed that after a merger, Singapore would retain autonomy in labour, education and health, among others, unlike the other states in the Federation of Malaya. In exchange, Singapore received an underproportioned representation in the [House of Representatives of Parliament](/wiki/Dewan_Rakyat "Dewan Rakyat"). Singapore within Malaysia was seen as having a special status (similar to [Northern Ireland](/wiki/Northern_Ireland "Northern Ireland") in the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom")) and was thus not grouped with the other non-autonomous states in the Peninsula. Although this arrangement was brief and Singapore was ultimately expelled from the Federation two years later in 1965, becoming a fully sovereign country, the Interpretation Act 1965 of the Parliament of Singapore still defines Malaya as comprising the States of Malaya and Singapore in a geographical sense.[[4]](#cite_note-7) Today, the States of Malaya are colloquially referred to as Peninsular Malaysia and West Malaysia, excluding the Borneo States and [Singapore](/wiki/Singapore "Singapore"). The term should also not be confused with the [Malay Peninsula](/wiki/Malay_Peninsula "Malay Peninsula"), which includes lands that are a part of [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar") and [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand").

## Terminology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Peninsular_Malaysia&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Terminology")]

**Peninsular Malaysia (States of Malaya)** comprises the states of [Johor](/wiki/Johor "Johor"), [Kedah](/wiki/Kedah "Kedah"), [Kelantan](/wiki/Kelantan "Kelantan"), [Malacca](/wiki/Malacca "Malacca"), [Negeri Sembilan](/wiki/Negeri_Sembilan "Negeri Sembilan"), [Pahang](/wiki/Pahang "Pahang"), [Penang](/wiki/Penang "Penang"), [Perak](/wiki/Perak "Perak"), [Perlis](/wiki/Perlis "Perlis"), [Selangor](/wiki/Selangor "Selangor"), and [Terengganu](/wiki/Terengganu "Terengganu"), as well as the federal territories of [Kuala Lumpur](/wiki/Kuala_Lumpur "Kuala Lumpur") and [Putrajaya](/wiki/Putrajaya "Putrajaya").

**Malaya** comprises Peninsular Malaysia and the [Republic of Singapore](/wiki/Singapore "Singapore").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

**Malay Peninsula** comprises the [southern tip](/wiki/Kawthaung_District "Kawthaung District") of [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar"), Peninsular Malaysia, and [Southern Thailand](/wiki/Southern_Thailand "Southern Thailand").

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Peninsular_Malaysia&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Demographics")]

Further information: [Demographics of Malaysia](/wiki/Demographics_of_Malaysia "Demographics of Malaysia")

Ethnicity in Peninsular Malaysia (2020)

1. Bumiputera (Malay) (66.0%)
2. Chinese (24.0%)
3. Indian (9.00%)
4. Indigenous (Aslian) / Non-Malay Bumiputera (0.30%)
5. Others (0.70%)

Religion in Peninsular Malaysia – 2020 est.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Religion | |  | Percent | |
| [Islam](/wiki/Islam "Islam") | |  | 67% | |
| [Buddhism](/wiki/Buddhism "Buddhism") | |  | 21.4% | |
| [Hinduism](/wiki/Hinduism "Hinduism") | |  | 7.6% | |
| [Christianity](/wiki/Christianity "Christianity") | |  | 3.2% | |
| [Chinese folk religion](/wiki/Chinese_folk_religion "Chinese folk religion") | |  | 0.9% | |
| Others | |  | 1.7% | |

Peninsular Malaysia is largely inhabited by [ethnic Malays](/wiki/Malays_(ethnic_group) "Malays (ethnic group)"), predominantly [Muslims](/wiki/Muslim "Muslim") at about 65–67%.[[5]](#cite_note-8) However, large Chinese and Indian populations exist. The [Orang Asli](/wiki/Orang_Asli "Orang Asli") are the indigenous people of Peninsular Malaysia; in 2022, they numbered around 209,575 and mostly lived in inland and rural parts of the region.[[6]](#cite_note-9)

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Peninsular_Malaysia&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Economy")]

As of 2012, Peninsular Malaysia oil production stood at 520,000 [barrel of oil equivalent](/wiki/Barrel_of_oil_equivalent "Barrel of oil equivalent") per day.[[7]](#cite_note-10)

## Other features

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Peninsular_Malaysia&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Other features")]

### East Coast and West Coast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Peninsular_Malaysia&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: East Coast and West Coast")]

The term *East Coast* ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Pantai Timur*; [Jawi](/wiki/Jawi_script "Jawi script"): ڤنتاي تيمور) is particularly used in Malaysia to describe the following states in Peninsular Malaysia facing the [South China Sea](/wiki/South_China_Sea "South China Sea"), a [marginal sea](/wiki/List_of_seas#Marginal_seas_by_ocean "List of seas") of the [Pacific Ocean](/wiki/Pacific_Ocean "Pacific Ocean"):

* [Kelantan](/wiki/Kelantan "Kelantan")
* [Pahang](/wiki/Pahang "Pahang")
* [Terengganu](/wiki/Terengganu "Terengganu")

The term *West Coast* ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Pantai Barat*; [Jawi](/wiki/Jawi_script "Jawi script"): ڤنتاي بارت) refers informally to a collection of states in Peninsular Malaysia situated towards the western coast generally facing the [Strait of Malacca](/wiki/Strait_of_Malacca "Strait of Malacca") which is a component of the [Indian Ocean](/wiki/Indian_Ocean "Indian Ocean"), as opposed to the East Coast. The West Coast is partitioned further into three regions:

* *Central Region*
  + [Kuala Lumpur](/wiki/Kuala_Lumpur "Kuala Lumpur") ([federal territory](/wiki/Federal_Territory_(Malaysia) "Federal Territory (Malaysia)"))
  + [Putrajaya](/wiki/Putrajaya "Putrajaya") ([federal territory](/wiki/Federal_Territory_(Malaysia) "Federal Territory (Malaysia)"))
  + [Selangor](/wiki/Selangor "Selangor")
* *Northern Region*
  + [Kedah](/wiki/Kedah "Kedah")
  + [Penang](/wiki/Penang "Penang")
  + [Perak](/wiki/Perak "Perak")
  + [Perlis](/wiki/Perlis "Perlis")
* *Southern Region*
  + [Johor](/wiki/Johor "Johor")
  + [Melaka](/wiki/Melaka "Melaka")
  + [Negeri Sembilan](/wiki/Negeri_Sembilan "Negeri Sembilan")

Although Johor has a coastline facing the [South China Sea](/wiki/South_China_Sea "South China Sea") on the [Pacific Ocean](/wiki/Pacific_Ocean "Pacific Ocean"), it is not generally regarded as an East Coast state, since the main coastline of the state is located on the [Straits of Johor](/wiki/Straits_of_Johor "Straits of Johor") of the [Indian Ocean](/wiki/Indian_Ocean "Indian Ocean").

The East Coast of Peninsular Malaysia is socioculturally different in terms of overall racial composition and political affiliations compared to the West Coast – the states' demographics in the former are overwhelmingly [Malay](/wiki/Malaysian_Malays "Malaysian Malays") and its people lean towards [social conservative](/wiki/Social_conservative "Social conservative") and [Islamist](/wiki/Islamist "Islamist") values, their electoral representation dominated by the [Malaysian Islamic Party](/wiki/Malaysian_Islamic_Party "Malaysian Islamic Party").[[8]](#cite_note-11)

### West Malaysia and East Malaysia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Peninsular_Malaysia&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: West Malaysia and East Malaysia")]

The distinction between West and [East Malaysia](/wiki/East_Malaysia "East Malaysia") (Sabah and Sarawak) goes beyond the sphere of [geography](/wiki/Geography "Geography"). Being separate regions administratively before the formation of the Malaysia, there exists more [autonomy](/wiki/Self-governance "Self-governance") than the original States of Malaya, e.g. in having a different judicial court structure and separate immigration regulations. These rights were granted as part of Sarawak's [18-point agreement](/wiki/18-point_agreement "18-point agreement") and Sabah's [20-point agreement](/wiki/20-point_agreement "20-point agreement") with the [Federation of Malaya](/wiki/Federation_of_Malaya "Federation of Malaya") during the formation of expanded federation.
